examples.title: Algorithm: Calculate Mean
examples.description: Calculates the mean of a given dataset
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CalculateMean
INPUT: dataset (array of numbers)
OUTPUT: mean (number)

BEGIN
 sum = 0
 FOR EACH value IN dataset
 sum = sum + value
 END FOR
 mean = sum / LENGTH(dataset)
 RETURN mean
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode calculates the mean of a given dataset by summing up all the values and dividing by the number of values.

examples.title: Python: Calculate Median
examples.description: Calculates the median of a given dataset
examples.code:
def calculate_median(dataset):
 sorted_dataset = sorted(dataset)
 n = len(sorted_dataset)
 if n % 2 == 0:
 median = (sorted_dataset[n // 2 - 1] + sorted_dataset[n // 2]) / 2
 else:
 median = sorted_dataset[n // 2]
 return median

# Example usage:
dataset = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
median = calculate_median(dataset)
print(median) # Output: 3
examples.explanation: This Python function calculates the median of a given dataset by sorting the values and finding the middle value.

examples.title: Python: Visualize Histogram
examples.description: Visualizes a histogram of a given dataset
examples.code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def visualize_histogram(dataset):
 plt.hist(dataset, bins=10)
 plt.xlabel('Value')
 plt.ylabel('Frequency')
 plt.title('Histogram of Dataset')
 plt.show()

# Example usage:
dataset = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
visualize_histogram(dataset)
examples.explanation: This Python function visualizes a histogram of a given dataset using matplotlib.
